---
title: "ERIM Logistic Regression"
format: html
---
## Load and clean data
```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

myData = pd.read_excel("/Users/danielkwong/Downloads/Big_Data_Files.xlsx", sheet_name = 'ERIMData')

#Making ‘Dogs’ and ‘Cats’ binary
myData['Dogs'] = myData['Dogs'].clip(lower = 0, upper = 1)
myData['Cats'] = myData['Cats'].clip(lower = 0, upper = 1)

#Binning income levels
myData['HHInc_binned'] = pd.cut(myData['HHInc'],
                            bins=[0, 4, 10, 14],
                            labels=['low', 'mid', 'high'])
#Creating dummy variables for each categorical variable
myData = pd.get_dummies(myData, columns= ['ResType', 'ResStatus','HHInc_binned', 'M_Rel', 'Cable', 'Cats', 'Dogs'], drop_first=True)

print(myData.head())
print(myData.shape)
print(myData.columns)
```

We are using **logistic regression** to capture purchase decision for **yogurt** and **frozen dinner** based on information about the household from the ERIM dataset. This means that we will be setting the cutoff for the logistic regression models at **YogExp > 0** and **DinExp > 0**. 

## Create the binary target variable
```{python}
# yogurt purchase indicator
threshold = myData["YogExp"].median()

myData["YogBuyer"] = (myData["YogExp"] > threshold).astype(int)

print(threshold)
print(myData[["YogExp", "YogBuyer"]].sample(10))
```

## Select predictor variables 
```{python}
predictors = [
'HHInc', 'HHNbr', 'MWrkHrs', 'MEdu', 'FWrkHrs', 'FEdu', 'F_Rel', 'ResType_2', 'ResType_3', 'ResType_4', 'ResType_5', 'ResType_6', 'ResStatus_2', 'ResStatus_3', 'HHInc_binned_mid', 'HHInc_binned_high', 'M_Rel_2', 'Cable_1', 'Cats_1', 'Dogs_1'
]
X = myData[predictors]
```

## Define target variable 
```{python}
yYog = myData["YogBuyer"]
```

## Train/test split
```{python}
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

Xtrain, Xtest, yYogTrain, yYogTest = train_test_split(
    X, yYog, test_size=0.3, random_state=1
)
```

## Fit logistic regression model
```{python}
from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression

yogModel = LogisticRegression(max_iter=10000)
yogModel.fit(Xtrain, yYogTrain)
```

## Predict probability 
```{python}
yogProb = yogModel.predict_proba(Xtest)[:,1]
```

## Predict class
```{python}
yogPred = yogModel.predict(Xtest)
```

## Model evaluation
```{python}
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix, accuracy_score, roc_auc_score

print("Yogurt Model")
print(confusion_matrix(yYogTest, yogPred))
print("Accuracy:", accuracy_score(yYogTest, yogPred))
print("AUC:", roc_auc_score(yYogTest, yogProb))
```

## ROC curve graph
```{python}
from sklearn.metrics import roc_curve, auc
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# compute ROC value
fprYog, tprYog, _ = roc_curve(yYogTest, yogProb)

# compute AUC
aucYog = auc(fprYog, tprYog)

# plot
plt.figure(figsize=(7,6))

plt.plot(fprYog, tprYog, label=f"Yogurt Model (AUC = {aucYog:.3f})")

# random classifier line
plt.plot([0,1], [0,1], linestyle="--")

plt.xlabel("False Positive Rate")
plt.ylabel("True Positive Rate")
plt.title("ROC Curve – Logistic Regression Models")
plt.legend()

plt.show()
```

## Feature importance for Yogurt Purchase
```{python}
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

# get coefficients
coef = pd.Series(yogModel.coef_[0], index=X.columns)
coef = coef.sort_values()

# color bars based on sign
colors = ["darkred" if c < 0 else "steelblue" for c in coef]

plt.figure(figsize=(8,12))

bars = plt.barh(coef.index, coef.values, color=colors)

# x-axis ticks every 0.1
xmin = coef.min() - 0.1
xmax = coef.max() + 0.1
ticks = np.arange(round(xmin,1), round(xmax,1)+0.1, 0.1)
plt.xticks(ticks)

# vertical dotted gridlines
plt.grid(axis="x", linestyle=":", color="lightgrey")

# bold vertical zero line
plt.axvline(0, color="black", linewidth=1)

# add coefficient labels
for i, v in enumerate(coef.values):
    
    if v >= 0:
        plt.text(v + 0.02, i, f"{v:.2f}", va="center")
    else:
        plt.text(v - 0.02, i, f"{v:.2f}", va="center", ha="right")

plt.xlabel("Coefficient Value")
plt.title("Drivers of Yogurt Purchase")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```
